{{Quote|Be careful! How would it look to Lord Farfalla if his envoy were killed?|The envoy greets Petja|<ref name="JvS" />}}
A winged [[Gender/Legends|female]] '''envoy''' was in the service of [[Jedi Lord]] [[Valenthyne Farfalla]] during the [[Ruusan campaign]] against the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]]. The charismatic Farfalla had departed [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] to recruit more [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] to join Lord [[Hoth (Jedi Lord)|Hoth's]] [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]]. He returned to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] with [[Farfalla's Knights|three hundred Knights and soldiers]], including the Envoy, only to find Hoth resentful of the long absence. When a stalemate occurred between the two Jedi, the Envoy used her own initiative to present an apology to Hoth, thus restoring the relationship between the two parties. She then fought in a number of battles on Ruusan, which eventually saw the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan|defeat]] of the Brotherhood.

==Biography==
{{Quote|One day…one day I'll pluck every one of your feathers and tickle you with them!|Lord Farfalla to the Envoy|<ref name="JvS" />}}
The Envoy was a member of a winged species during the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Republic Dark Age]]. She was one of [[Farfalla's Knights|three hundred soldiers]] recruited by the charismatic [[Jedi Lord]] [[Valenthyne Farfalla]] to join Lord [[Hoth (Jedi Lord)|Hoth's]] forces on [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]],<ref name="JvS" /> a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Teraab sector/Legends|Teraab sector]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="KKT">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/kylekatarnstale5|text=Light and Dark: Kyle Katarn's Tale, Mission 5|int=Kyle Katarn's Tale}}</ref> Lord Hoth had assembled a large force of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] known as the [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]], and was engaged in a drawn out [[Ruusan campaign|campaign]] against the forces of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]].<ref name="JvS" /> Reaching a stalemate, Lord Farfalla had volunteered to recruit more [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] to aid their Army.<ref name="Path">''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]''</ref>

[[File:Envoy1.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The envoy brings her message to Lord Hoth.]]
The envoy and the other Knights were part of a flotilla, containing a number of [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Gunship/Legends|gunships]] spearheaded by Farfalla's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''[[Fairwind]]''.<ref name="Path" /> Together, they defeated the treacherous [[Lahzar]], before returning to the [[Ruusan system/Legends|Ruusan system]],<ref name="JvS" /> where they were faced with a Sith blockade.<ref name="Path" /> The Knights were able to rescue the Army of Light from [[FC-20 speeder bike/Legends|Sith speeders]] by making a daring salvo from the ''Fairwind''. However, they received a hostile reception from Lord Hoth who was still bitter about his many losses against the Sith. He blamed Farfalla for arriving too late, a complaint deriving from his long rivalry and dislike of Farfalla.<ref name="JvS" />

This led to a stalemate between the two parties, with both the Jedi Lords refusing each other's help. Seeing that Lord Hoth's forces were walking into a trap and that her Lord was too stubborn to help, the envoy took matters into her own hands. Flying to the Army of Light, she claimed to bring a message from Lord Farfalla. Along with military information gathered on Hoth's forces, she brought an "apology" from Farfalla.<ref name="JvS" /> Hoth himself had received a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] of his close friend [[Pernicar]] the night prior, and awoke determined to repair his rift with Farfalla.<ref name="Path" /> Hoth received the apology, believing it to be sincere, and reciprocated.<ref name="JvS" />

Returning to the ''Fairwind'', the envoy delivered Hoth's apology. Farfalla temporarily believed it, before, much amused, seeing through it. Nevertheless, he engaged the Sith forces, overwhelming them in the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan]], while Hoth went to engage [[Sith Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Skere Kaan]] in a cave system. When Kaan unleashed his [[thought bomb]], wiping out Hoth's forces, the envoy survived, having retreated with the rest of the Knights from the range of the bomb.<ref name="JvS" />

==Personality and traits==
[[File:Envoy_long.jpg|thumb|right|130px|The envoy.]]
The envoy was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] of a [[Species/Legends|species]] with both [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] and avian characteristics. Her upper [[Limb/Legends|limbs]] were wings covered with [[Color/Legends|brown]] feathers, fully capable of flying—her primary form of locomotion. Her lower limbs were also avian and were able to grasp and manipulate items. The Envoy was very agile, able to pluck [[Arrow/Legends|arrows]] out of the air, as occurred when Petja accidentally shot at her. She had two small horns on her forehead, with long flame-red [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]].<ref name="JvS" />

The envoy typically wore a gold earring in her pointed humanoid [[Ear/Legends|ears]] and wore a tunic colored in the red and gold livery common among Farfalla's forces. She utilized the conventional weaponry of common folk, wielding a short [[Sword/Legends|sword]] during the battle. The envoy was on good personal terms with Farfalla, and had initiative enough to seek a solution to the stalemate between Farfalla and Hoth, without seeking permission from her Lord.<ref name="JvS" />

==Behind the scenes==
The envoy was created for the [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] ''[[Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith]]'', appearing in issues [[Jedi vs. Sith 5|#5]] and [[Jedi vs. Sith 5|#6]]. The comic, written by [[Darko Macan]] and pencilled by [[Ramón F. Bachs]], was released in [[2001]]. However, she went unnamed in both of these issues. The envoy again appeared in [[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction|''Path of Destruction'']] ([[2006]]) written by [[Drew Karpyshyn]], and possibly [[Darth Bane: Rule of Two|''Rule of Two'']] ([[2007]]). In ''Path of Destruction'', Karpyshyn rewrote some of the events depicted in ''Jedi vs. Sith'', although none of the scenes involving the envoy's role directly contradict the comic, instead adding in Hoth's personal revelation prior to his encounter with the envoy.
